item.title: The Four Tops
item.description: Genres: Soul, Pop soul Scene: Detroit / Motown Records Era: 1953–present (classic era 1964–1967) RYM · Apple Music · Spotify Levi Stubbs had a voice built for crisis — a baritone-tenor that could move from conversational warmth to desperate, shouting intensity with an abruptness that made every song...

item.title: Holland-Dozier-Holland
item.description: Era: 1962–1967 (Motown); 1968–present (independent) Genres: Soul, Pop soul, Girl group Key collaborators: The Supremes, The Four Tops, The Temptations, Martha and the Vandellas RYM · Apple Music · Spotify The most prolific and commercially successful songwriting-production team in the history of Mot...

item.title: The Temptations
item.description: Genres: Soul, Pop soul, psychedelic soul Scene: Detroit / Motown Records Era: 1960–present (classic era 1964–1971) RYM · Apple Music · Spotify The Motown act that best illustrates the label’s capacity for reinvention.

item.title: Berry Gordy
item.description: Born: November 28, 1929 (Detroit, Michigan) Active: 1957–present (label-building era: 1959–1988) Role: Label founder, songwriter, producer Key institutions: Motown Records (1959–1988), Jobete Music (publishing), Motown Productions (film/television) Wikipedia · RYM The $800 that founded Tamla Records...

item.title: Motown Records
item.description: Founded: 1959 Location: Detroit, Michigan (2648 West Grand Boulevard — “Hitsville U.S.A.”) Founder: Berry Gordy Jr.
